(Photo supplied by RCMP) Court Less than 1 min.read # Pre-trial conference set for accused in drug bust in Williams Lake George Henderson By George Henderson July 20, 2026 FacebookXReddItWhatsAppEmail A 55-year old man, charged in connection with a drug bust in Williams Lake, is due back in court next month for a Pre-trial conference. Jason Bruneau is scheduled to appear in provincial court on August 12. He’s facing one count of possession for the purpose of trafficking. The charge is connection with an incident in January of 2024. Williams Lake RCMP, with the assistance of the Cariboo

## Arrest made year after fatal Okanagan Granfondo crash Published 4:26 pm Monday, July 20, 2026 **By Brennan Phillips** The Okanagan Granfondo is being discontinued after 14 years, event organizers announced on Oct. 2, 2025 ((File photo)) One year and a week after a crash during the Okanagan Granfondo killed an athlete and hospitalized two others, one man has been arrested and charged. The charges were approved by the BC Prosecution Service, and on Monday, July 20, Jonathan Crant was arrested. items.content_html:

## Effort to recall B.C. MLA Dallas Brodie falls short Published 4:09 pm Monday, July 20, 2026 One B.C. MLA Dallas Brodie speaks to media in the legislature on Thursday, Oct. 23, 2025\. (Mark Page/Black Press Media) The petition to recall Vancouver-Quilchena MLA Dallas Brodie, leader of the OneBC Party, has failed to gather the necessary number of signatures by a wide margin. For a petition to be successful, registered canvassers must collect signatures from 40 per cent or more of the 15,232 people who were eligible to vote in the Vancouver-Quilchena riding on Oct. 19, 2024. Petitioners

## Former B.C. Liberal cabinet minister Mike Bernier to lead CentreBC party Published 3:26 pm Monday, July 20, 2026 Mike Bernier introduced himself as the new leader of CentreBC in Victoria on Saturday, July 18, 2026\. (Photo courtesy John Yanyshyn/CentreBC) Former B.C. Liberal cabinet minister Mike Bernier is the new leader of the CentreBC party. Bernier was a member of the B.C. legislature for 11 years, representing the Liberal party through its rebranding to B.C. United in 2023\. He is also the former mayor of Dawson Creek and spent 20 years working in the oil and gas industry. Founding

## BCWS responding to 13 out of control wildfires throughout Cariboo Fire Centre Published 12:38 pm Sunday, July 19, 2026 **By Patrick Davies** 1/2 The French Bar Creek wildfire on Saturday, July 18\. (BC Wildfire Service) The BC Wildfire Service continues to respond to 32 active wildfires in the Cariboo Fire Centre on Sunday, July 19, 13 of which are out of control. Most of the out-of-control wildfires were discovered in the last two days, following thundershowers which rolled through the Cariboo Fire Centre on Friday, July 17\. They range in size from a few hectares to a few thousand. The

## Decades-long journey brings Anita de Koning to Ashcroft Published 10:00 am Saturday, July 18, 2026 **By Cliff Marsh** Anita de Koning has been active with the Ashcroft-Cache Creek Seniors' Centre for many years. (Barbara Roden/Ashcroft-Cache Creek Journal) Anita de Koning made an epic journey to get to Ashcroft. The Hague, South Holland is where she was born into the turmoil of World War II, but she was fortunate to be too young to fully understand the atrocities of that time. In 1953, drawn to the allure of Canada and the seemingly limitless opportunities to nurture his family, her father

## Investigation into injuries sustained during Kamloops arrest ongoing: IIO Published 11:30 am Thursday, July 16, 2026 **By Josh Fischlin** The Independent Investigations Office of B.C. (File photo) B.C.’s police watchdog continues to investigate an incident that occurred in Kamloops in May, which resulted in injuries to a woman. According to a July 16 press release issued by the Independent Investigations Office (IIO) of BC, the incident happened during an arrest in Kamloops on May 4. The IIO said an off-duty RCMP officer was in a store in the 2100-block of Trans-Canada Hwy East when he saw
